description_long: This object consists of two large solar panel arrays mounted on metal posts, positioned on a patch of uneven grassy terrain. Each array is composed of multiple blue photovoltaic modules arranged in a rectangular grid, supported by a robust metallic frame. The panels are elevated above the ground, allowing for optimal sunlight exposure and minimizing shading from the surrounding landscape. The mounting structure appears to be made of steel or aluminum, providing stability and durability. The terrain beneath the panels is slightly mounded and covered in green grass, suggesting an outdoor installation typical of solar farms or urban green spaces. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present on the exterior of the installation.
description: Two large blue solar panel arrays on metal posts, elevated above grassy terrain, with a sturdy metallic frame and no visible cabling or inverters.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the metallic frames and support posts of the two solar panel arrays, with grassy terrain beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile and tilt of the solar panels, as well as the height of the supporting posts.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the blue photovoltaic surfaces of both solar panel arrays, clearly showing their grid arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the angled orientation of the panels and the supporting structure's depth.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted, supported by steel or aluminum posts and frame
attribute_array_configuration: two separate rectangular arrays, each with a 3x4 grid of panels, arranged side by side
